title: Seed-list model + TOML loader (S3 fetch groundwork)
description:
Session 3 (Fetch) groundwork: the **seed-list model + config loader**. This ticket builds the `Seed` data model and a loader **only** — **no fetching, no adapters, no network**. Source adapters (a later ticket, after the fetch contract merges) consume `Seed`s and expand them into concrete fetch targets; they are out of scope here.

## Context

The owner provides a **seed list of sources** (`REPO_CONTENT.md` -> user journey: "the owner provides a seed list of sources and an applicant profile"). Session 3 turns that list into fetch targets. This ticket is the typed representation of a seed + a loader from a config file — the input end of the fetch pipeline, independent of the fetcher itself.

## Locked decisions (honor; see `REPO_CONTENT.md`)

- Seed source types named in the product: **Instagram / X accounts, subreddits, Devpost, scholarship platforms** (plain URLs). The **IG/X auth-walled types are deferred** to their own later session, **but the model must still represent them** (a `type` enum covering all of them) — only the *adapters* (later) decide which types they actually fetch. Represent all; fetch a subset.
- **Single-user; no auth.** Keep the model minimal — no per-seed credentials/schedule in v1.

## Scope

1. A `Seed` pydantic v2 model:
   - `type` — enum covering `url`, `reddit`, `devpost`, `instagram`, `x` (extendable); unknown type rejected.
   - `value` — the handle / subreddit / URL for that type.
   - `enabled: bool = True`, and an optional `label`/`note` (nullable).
2. A loader that reads a seed list from a **TOML** config file (stdlib `tomllib`, Python 3.11+ — no new dep) into `list[Seed]`, validating each entry (malformed entry -> clear error; empty file -> empty list).
3. **No fetching, no adapter logic, no network.**
acceptance_criteria: `Seed` exists with `type` (from the defined enum incl. the auth-walled types), `value`, `enabled` (default `True`), optional `label`; constructing a valid seed works and an unknown `type` is rejected.
The loader parses a sample TOML config into `list[Seed]`; a malformed entry raises a clear error; an empty config yields an empty list.
Unit tests cover: model construction (valid + invalid type), loader round-trip from a **temporary** config file, and the empty + malformed cases. No network.
